rows.description: of Mr. Holsworthy from an unknown hand
rows.notes: Monetary columns clearly read 5 | 0 | 0. The descriptive phrase is fairly legible: 'of Mr Holsworthy from an unknown hand' — some letters in the surname are slightly compressed but consistent with 'Holsworthy'. High confidence in amounts and good confidence in wording.

This appears to be a small internal section header (no amounts). The first word reads as 'Increment' or 'Increments' but the middle letters are faint; 'Battley' is a plausible reading of the second word. Classified as header because it introduces a subsequent set of lines and has no amounts.
No monetary amount. The text looks like an abbreviation 'Gent. Comd' or 'Gent Comd' (possibly 'Gent Committee' abbreviated). Slight ambiguity in final letters due to light ink.
Clear alignment and clear amounts 10 | 0 | 0. Description 'Act Support' is legible and corresponds to a payment/support item. Low ambiguity.

This is a final total line — the large sum at the right reads most clearly as '210' in the pounds column and '02' in the shillings column. The pence area appears as '06' but there is an additional small stray mark or flourish to the right (looks like a '9' or pen flourish). Interpreted as 6 pence with no recognised fractional symbol; fraction is left blank. Because of the stray mark and slight smudging around the pence, confidence is moderate (0.5). The left description reads 'Comr: Library' with 'Sum' or 'Sumn' nearby; that is also somewhat uncertain but clearly a total line.
